title: Bed Reassignment Causes Patient Context Drift
scenario: A bedside execution workflow relies on bed assignment, but the bed is reassigned before the action executes.
initial_state: The clinical action was prepared while the patient occupied the recorded bed.
state_transition: The bed was reassigned to a different patient while the execution workflow retained the original location context.
execution_boundary: The bedside clinical action is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Location context cannot substitute for patient identity continuity.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because bed-location context drift breaks the patient identity binding.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must be blocked when location-derived identity no longer preserves patient continuity.
